title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text: 🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
Agenda (~60 min)



Time
Activity




⏱️ 5′
0. Check your setup


⏱️ 10′
1. Your first document (HTML + PDF)


⏱️ 15′
2. Prose + code + data


⏱️ 10′
3. The reproducible payoff (change the data)


⏱️ 10′
4. Slides from the same source


⏱️ 10′
5. Q&A & next steps



Download the sample data: data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv (from the workshop repo). Put it in your working folder under a data/ subfolder.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 0 · Check your setup (5′)
Create a new working folder, open it in RStudio, then in the Terminal:
quarto check
Look for (✓) for Quarto and LaTeX. (If using Python: also for Jupyter.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 1 · Your first document (10′)
Create hello.qmd:
---
title: "Hello Quarto"
format: html
---

## Welcome
A normal paragraph: **bold**, *italic*, `code`. A list:

- one
- two
Click Render (or quarto render hello.qmd). Then change format: html → format: pdf and render again. Same source, two outputs. 🎉

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 2 · Prose + code + data (15′)
The heart of Quarto: code runs at render time and its results flow into the document. Create analysis.qmd (pick R or Python):

RPython


---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)
df <- read.csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]
```

Mean poverty in 2023 = **`r round(mean(df23$kemiskinan), 2)`%**.

```{r}
df |> group_by(tahun) |> summarise(rata = mean(kemiskinan)) |>
  ggplot(aes(tahun, rata)) + geom_line() + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Poverty (%)")
```


---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
print(f"Mean 2023: {df23['kemiskinan'].mean():.2f}%")
```

```{python}
df.groupby("tahun")["kemiskinan"].mean().plot(marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Year"); plt.ylabel("Poverty (%)"); plt.show()
```



Render. Notice: the inline number and the chart both come from the data — no copy-paste.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 3 · The reproducible payoff (10′)
The “aha” moment:

Open data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv.
Change Kota Semarang 2023 from 4.58 → 24.58. Save.
Re-render analysis.qmd.

The mean and the chart change instantly. One data edit → the whole document follows.






ImportantMust know: quarto render, not just preview



The live preview (on localhost) does not watch data files. After editing data, run quarto render in the Terminal and open the result — or edit-and-save a .qmd to make the preview refresh. (This is the most common gotcha.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 4 · Slides from the same source (10′)
No rewriting needed. Edit the header of analysis.qmd to add two slide formats:
---
title: "Central Java Poverty"
format:
  revealjs: default     # HTML slides (screen/web)
  beamer:               # PDF slides (projector)
    aspectratio: 169
---
Then:
quarto render analysis.qmd --to revealjs   # -> analysis.html
quarto render analysis.qmd --to beamer     # -> analysis.pdf
Each ## heading becomes a slide; your chart appears on a slide — still from the same data.

title: Hands-on: Quarto in 1 Hour
text:
⏱️ 5 · Done & next steps (10′)
You just: wrote a document, ran analysis inside it, proved reproducibility, and made slides — all from one source.
Keep going:

📘 Book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (complete, Chapter I–V, ready templates; Indonesian) — https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
📦 Thesis templates (R & Python) + defense slides — in the book’s repo.
📑 Quarto documentation — https://quarto.org/docs/guide/
🔖 Citations via Zotero — https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text: 🌐 English version: read this in English

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
Agenda (±60 menit)



Waktu
Kegiatan




⏱️ 5′
0. Cek kesiapan


⏱️ 10′
1. Dokumen pertama (HTML + PDF)


⏱️ 15′
2. Tulisan + kode + data


⏱️ 10′
3. Bukti reproducible (ubah data)


⏱️ 10′
4. Slide dari data yang sama


⏱️ 10′
5. Tanya jawab & langkah lanjut



Unduh data contoh: data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv (dari repositori workshop). Taruh di folder kerja Anda, di dalam subfolder data/.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 0 · Cek kesiapan (5′)
Buat folder kerja baru, buka di RStudio, lalu di Terminal:
quarto check
Cari (✓) untuk Quarto dan LaTeX. (Bila pakai Python: juga untuk Jupyter.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 1 · Dokumen pertama (10′)
Buat halo.qmd:
---
title: "Halo Quarto"
format: html
---

## Selamat datang
Paragraf biasa: **tebal**, *miring*, `kode`. Daftar:

- satu
- dua
Tekan Render (atau quarto render halo.qmd). Lalu ganti format: html → format: pdf, render lagi. Isi sama, dua keluaran. 🎉

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 2 · Tulisan + kode + data (15′)
Inti Quarto: kode dijalankan saat render; hasilnya masuk ke dokumen. Buat analisis.qmd (pilih R atau Python):

RPython


---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(dplyr); library(ggplot2)
df <- read.csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 <- df[df$tahun == 2023, ]
```

Rata-rata kemiskinan 2023 = **`r round(mean(df23$kemiskinan), 2)`%**.

```{r}
df |> group_by(tahun) |> summarise(rata = mean(kemiskinan)) |>
  ggplot(aes(tahun, rata)) + geom_line() + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Tahun", y = "Kemiskinan (%)")
```


---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv("data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv")
df23 = df[df["tahun"] == 2023]
print(f"Rata-rata 2023: {df23['kemiskinan'].mean():.2f}%")
```

```{python}
df.groupby("tahun")["kemiskinan"].mean().plot(marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Tahun"); plt.ylabel("Kemiskinan (%)"); plt.show()
```



Render. Perhatikan: angka di kalimat dan grafik sama-sama dari data — tanpa salin-tempel.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 3 · Bukti reproducible (10′)
Momen “aha”:

Buka data/data_kemiskinan_jateng.csv.
Ubah Kota Semarang 2023 dari 4.58 → 24.58. Simpan.
Render ulang analisis.qmd.

Angka rata-rata dan grafik langsung berubah. Satu edit data → seluruh dokumen ikut.






PentingWajib tahu: quarto render, bukan sekadar preview



Pratinjau langsung (live preview di localhost) tidak memantau berkas data. Setelah mengubah data, jalankan quarto render di Terminal lalu buka hasilnya — atau ubah-simpan sebuah .qmd agar pratinjau menyegarkan. (Ini jebakan paling umum.)

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 4 · Slide dari data yang sama (10′)
Tak perlu menulis ulang. Ubah header analisis.qmd agar punya dua format slide:
---
title: "Kemiskinan Jawa Tengah"
format:
  revealjs: default     # slide HTML (layar/web)
  beamer:               # slide PDF (proyektor)
    aspectratio: 169
---
Lalu:
quarto render analisis.qmd --to revealjs   # -> analisis.html
quarto render analisis.qmd --to beamer     # -> analisis.pdf
Judul ## menjadi satu slide; grafik tadi muncul di slide — tetap dari data yang sama.

title: Hands-on: Quarto dalam 1 Jam
text:
⏱️ 5 · Selesai & langkah lanjut (10′)
Anda baru saja: menulis dokumen, menjalankan analisis di dalamnya, membuktikan reproducibility, dan membuat slide — semua dari satu sumber.
Lanjutkan:

📘 Buku “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (lengkap, BAB I–V, templat siap pakai) — https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/
📦 Templat skripsi (R & Python) + slide sidang — di repositori buku.
📑 Dokumentasi Quarto — https://quarto.org/docs/guide/
🔖 Sitasi via Zotero — https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
🌐 English version: read this in English
Selamat datang! Workshop ini memperkenalkan Quarto — sistem penerbitan dokumen ilmiah yang menyatukan tulisan + kode + data dalam satu berkas, lalu menghasilkan PDF, HTML, Word, dan slide dari sumber yang sama.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Untuk siapa?


🎓 Mahasiswa (S1–S3)
Menyusun skripsi/tesis/disertasi kuantitatif: olah data, tabel, grafik, sitasi, dan PDF sesuai format kampus — tanpa salin-tempel dari SPSS/Excel ke Word.


🔬 Dosen & Peneliti
Membuat artikel, laporan, dan presentasi yang dapat diproduksi ulang. Data berubah? Cukup render ulang — angka, tabel, grafik, dan slide ikut diperbarui otomatis.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Masalah yang kita pecahkan







Alur lama (Word + SPSS/Excel)
Dengan Quarto




Angka disalin manual → rawan salah ketik
Angka muncul otomatis dari kode


Nomor tabel/gambar kacau saat menyisipkan
Penomoran & daftar isi otomatis


Revisi data = ulang olah + salin-tempel
Ganti data → render ulang sekali


Sitasi & daftar pustaka manual
Sitasi otomatis (mis. APA), via Zotero


Slide dibuat ulang terpisah
Slide dari data yang sama (Beamer/Reveal.js)

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Inti idenya
  berkas .qmd  ──►  Quarto  ──►  PDF · HTML · Word · Slides
 (tulisan +        (jalankan      (keluaran rapi,
  kode + data)      kode, rangkai)  reproducible)
Satu sumber, banyak keluaran. Ubah satu hal di data, dan semua hasil ikut berubah. Itulah reproducibility — dan itu yang akan kita buktikan langsung hari ini.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Mulai

👉 Ikuti tutorial hands-on — langkah demi langkah, bisa langsung Anda praktikkan.
🖥️ Unduh slide presentasi (PDF/Beamer)
📘 Buku lengkap “Quarto untuk Skripsi”: https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







TipYang perlu disiapkan



R + RStudio (RStudio sudah membawa Quarto), TinyTeX untuk PDF, dan opsional Python untuk contoh berbahasa Python. Detail di tutorial.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
🌐 Versi Bahasa Indonesia: baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia
Welcome! This workshop introduces Quarto — a scientific publishing system that unites prose + code + data in a single file and renders to PDF, HTML, Word, and slides from one source.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Who is this for?


🎓 Students
Writing a quantitative thesis/dissertation: data analysis, tables, figures, citations, and a campus-formatted PDF — without copy-pasting from SPSS/Excel into Word.


🔬 Lecturers & Researchers
Producing reproducible papers, reports, and presentations. Data changed? Just re-render — numbers, tables, figures, and slides update automatically.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
The problem we solve







The old way (Word + SPSS/Excel)
With Quarto




Numbers copied by hand → typos
Numbers appear automatically from code


Table/figure numbering breaks on insert
Numbering & table of contents automatic


Revising data = redo analysis + re-paste
Change data → re-render once


Manual citations & bibliography
Automatic citations (e.g. APA), via Zotero


Slides rebuilt separately
Slides from the same data (Beamer/Reveal.js)

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
The core idea
  .qmd file   ──►  Quarto  ──►  PDF · HTML · Word · Slides
 (prose +         (run the      (clean, reproducible
  code + data)     code, weave)   output)
One source, many outputs. Change one thing in the data, and everything updates. That’s reproducibility — and we’ll prove it live today.

title: Quarto Workshop
text:
Get started

👉 Follow the hands-on tutorial — step by step, do it yourself.
🖥️ Download the slides (PDF/Beamer)
📘 Full book “Quarto untuk Skripsi” (Indonesian): https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/







TipWhat to install



R + RStudio (RStudio ships with Quarto), TinyTeX for PDF, and optionally Python for the Python examples. Details in the tutorial.
